title: Exelixis — Projects
html:
Exelixis Active Projects & Workstreams

Governing Thought

The Exelixis project portfolio tells a clear story of a maturing relationship: four completed cabozantinib-era engagements (PET, Integrated Tracking, AE Training, Perxcept) laid the foundation, and the account has now inflected into a zanzalintinib pre-launch phase with one active multi-wave study and two live proposals. The current moment is pivotal — the Zanzalintinib Unbranded Campaign Impact study is the active proof point, while the ATU RFP and Branded Campaign Testing proposal are competitive bids that will determine whether ZoomRx becomes embedded in the zanzalintinib launch infrastructure or remains a one-project vendor. Delivery quality on the active campaign impact work is directly influencing the outcome of both pending proposals.

Account Health Dashboard

# Project Brand Service Status Last Activity Key Signal
04 Zanzalintinib Unbranded Campaign Impact Zanzalintinib Campaign Impact :green_circle: Active Feb 2026 (Wave 01 + 02) Multi-wave design signals tracking commitment; quality of this deliverable directly influences ATU and campaign testing decisions
-- Zanzalintinib ATU Zanzalintinib ATU :blue_circle: RFP Apr 2026 Formal RFP received (v1.0); competitive evaluation likely in progress
-- Zanzalintinib Branded Campaign Testing Zanzalintinib Campaign Testing :blue_circle: Proposal Apr 2026 Proposal v0.1 and v0.2 submitted; iterative dialogue signals engagement
03 Exelixis Perxcept CABOMETYX Perxcept :white_circle: Completed Jan 2024 ZoomRx proprietary method adopted — indicates methodological trust
02 Cabozantinib Integrated Tracking CABOMETYX Integrated Tracking :white_circle: Completed Oct 2023 Scope expansion from standalone PET to integrated tracking
01 EXL_Cabo_PET_2019 CABOMETYX PET :white_circle: Completed Oct 2023 (archived) Original engagement — foundation of the relationship
00 AE Training Corporate Training :white_circle: Completed Oct 2023 (archived) Non-standard engagement; shows breadth beyond core MR

Legend: :green_circle: Active/On Track | :blue_circle: New/Pipeline (RFP or Proposal) | :white_circle: Completed | :yellow_circle: Watch | :red_circle: Risk

Detailed Workstream Profiles

Active: Zanzalintinib Unbranded Campaign Impact (04)

Status: Active | Waves: 01 + 02 | Brand: Zanzalintinib | Service: Unbranded Campaign Impact

What it is: A multi-wave study measuring the impact of unbranded (disease awareness) campaigns as part of zanzalintinib pre-launch market shaping. Unbranded campaigns are a standard pre-launch tactic in oncology — they build disease awareness and treatment urgency among HCPs before a branded product is available.

Why it matters:

  • This is ZoomRx's first zanzalintinib-era project — the beachhead for all future work
  • Multi-wave design (Wave 01 + Wave 02) suggests a tracking commitment, not a one-off
  • The quality of this deliverable directly influences whether Exelixis awards the ATU and branded campaign testing to ZoomRx
  • Unbranded campaign insight can be leveraged to propose branded campaign testing as the natural next step ("Now that you've shaped the market, let's test your branded messages")

What to track:

  • Wave 01 delivery status and client feedback
  • Wave 02 timing and scope
  • Client satisfaction signals — is the insights team finding the output actionable?
  • Whether the campaign impact contact is the same person evaluating the ATU and branded testing proposals

Contact: [Populate from SOW/project emails]

Pipeline: Zanzalintinib ATU (RFP Stage)

Status: RFP received (Apr 2026) | Brand: Zanzalintinib | Service: ATU | Document: Exelixis RFP - zanzalintinib ATU v1.0

What it is: A formal request for proposal for an Awareness, Trial, and Usage tracking study for zanzalintinib. ATU is a standard pre-launch / early launch research tool that measures physician awareness of a new product, willingness to try it, and actual prescribing behavior over time.

Why it matters:

  • ATU is a foundational tracking study — the vendor who wins this likely runs it for 2-3+ years through launch
  • An RFP signals that Exelixis is evaluating multiple vendors — this is a competitive bid, not a sole-source award
  • Winning the ATU gives ZoomRx a recurring revenue stream and an ongoing relationship touchpoint that opens doors for PET, MSL/MEE, and Launch KPI proposals
  • Losing the ATU means another vendor gets the primary tracking relationship during launch — and ZoomRx would need to compete against an incumbent with live data

What to track:

  • RFP evaluation timeline — when does Exelixis plan to select a vendor?
  • Competitor vendors in the RFP process
  • Specific RFP requirements that ZoomRx can differentiate on (methodology, AI capabilities, integrated insights with campaign impact data)
  • Budget range indicated in the RFP
  • Evaluation criteria and weighting

Competitive positioning: The account team should leverage the active campaign impact work to differentiate — "ZoomRx already has context on zanzalintinib's market shaping efforts. Our ATU won't start from zero — it will build on the unbranded campaign insights to create a longitudinal pre-launch intelligence platform."

Contact: [Populate from RFP sender]

Pipeline: Zanzalintinib Branded Campaign Testing (Proposal Stage)

Status: Proposal submitted (v0.1 + v0.2) | Brand: Zanzalintinib | Service: Branded Campaign/Message Testing | Documents: Zanzalintinib Branded Campaign Testing Research Proposal v0.1, v0.2

What it is: Research to test zanzalintinib branded messages and campaign materials with HCPs before commercial launch. This helps Exelixis optimize its promotional messaging — ensuring that the messages reps deliver resonate with oncologists and drive prescribing intent.

Why it matters:

  • Two proposal versions (v0.1 to v0.2) indicate iterative refinement based on Exelixis feedback — this is an engaged dialogue, not a rejected pitch
  • Branded campaign testing is a direct upstream input to PET — "test the messages, then track how well they're delivered." Winning this creates a natural pipeline to PET
  • Combined with the ATU and unbranded campaign impact, this would give ZoomRx a three-legged pre-launch intelligence platform (market shaping effectiveness + market awareness + message optimization)

What to track:

  • Exelixis feedback on v0.2 — is a v0.3 expected, or is a decision imminent?
  • Competitive proposals from other vendors
  • Pricing feedback — is ZoomRx in the right range?
  • Timeline to decision
  • Whether this can be combined with ATU in a bundled proposal for better economics

Contact: [Populate from proposal correspondence]

Completed: Exelixis Perxcept (03)

Status: Completed | Date: Jan 2024 | Brand: CABOMETYX | Service: Perxcept (ZoomRx proprietary)

A specialty research engagement using ZoomRx's proprietary Perxcept methodology. The adoption of a ZoomRx-specific method signals trust in the firm's intellectual property and analytical approach. This is a proof point for selling other proprietary ZoomRx offerings to Exelixis.

[Populate with specific findings and impact from project archives.]

Completed: Cabozantinib Integrated Tracking (02)

Status: Completed | Date: Oct 2023 | Brand: CABOMETYX | Service: Integrated Tracking

An evolution from the original standalone PET to a broader integrated tracking engagement. The scope expansion from PET to integrated tracking suggests Exelixis saw sufficient value from the original PET to deepen the quantitative tracking relationship.

[Populate with specific findings and impact from project archives.]

Completed: EXL_Cabo_PET_2019 (01)

Status: Completed | Date: 2019 (archived Oct 2023) | Brand: CABOMETYX | Service: PET

The original engagement — a Cabozantinib Promotional Effectiveness Tracking study. This project established the ZoomRx-Exelixis relationship and built credibility in oncology promotional tracking. The PET methodology ZoomRx brought to cabozantinib is directly transferable to zanzalintinib.

[Populate with specific findings and impact from project archives.]

Completed: AE Training (00)

Status: Completed | Date: Oct 2023 (archived) | Service: Training

An adverse event training engagement. Non-standard for ZoomRx's core MR service portfolio, but demonstrates breadth and flexibility in serving Exelixis's needs beyond traditional market research.

Pipeline / Upcoming Work

Active Pipeline Summary

Opportunity Service Stage Est. Revenue Probability Timeline
Zanzalintinib ATU ATU RFP evaluation [Populate from proposal] Medium (competitive RFP) FY26 H1-H2
Zanzalintinib Branded Campaign Testing Message/Campaign Testing Proposal v0.2 submitted [Populate from proposal] Medium-High (engaged dialogue) FY26 H1-H2

Proposed Future Pipeline (Not Yet Pitched)

Opportunity Service Rationale When to Pitch Dependency
Zanzalintinib PET (launch tracking) PET ZoomRx's core service; ran cabo PET in 2019; natural extension of campaign testing ("test messages, then track delivery") After branded campaign testing win; or independently during launch planning Zanzalintinib regulatory timeline
Zanzalintinib Launch KPI Launch KPI Tracking Standard pre-launch/launch research that tracks key performance indicators (awareness, trial, share, satisfaction) longitudinally 6-12 months before expected approval ATU win creates natural conversation
MSL/MEE Tracking MSL/MEE Oncology MSLs are critical for TKI differentiation — scientific exchange drives prescribing in RCC/HCC When medical affairs contact is identified Contact map enrichment
HCP-Patient Conversations HCPP Understand real oncologist-patient conversations about TKI treatment — what drives treatment selection, switching, and adherence After ATU or PET establishes the tracking relationship Contact development
Digital Tracking Digital Tracking Monitor oncologist digital engagement with zanzalintinib content pre- and post-launch During launch planning phase Active project relationship established
OCE Tracking OCE Measure omnichannel engagement effectiveness for the zanzalintinib field force Post-launch Field force contact development
CABOMETYX Lifecycle Research Various (ATU, HCPP) CABOMETYX is still a $2B+ brand; lifecycle management research maintains revenue while zanzalintinib scales Anytime — parallel to zanzalintinib work Active relationship

Cross-Workstream Themes

1. The Cabo-to-Zanzalintinib Transition Is the Central Narrative

Every project on this account — past and future — maps to the cabozantinib-to-zanzalintinib franchise transition. The historical projects (PET, Integrated Tracking) served the mature franchise. The current and pipeline projects (campaign impact, ATU, branded testing) serve the pre-launch successor. Future opportunities (PET, Launch KPI, MSL) will serve the launch and post-launch phase. The account team should frame every proposal in the context of this transition.

2. The Current RFP Cycle Is a Defining Moment

The ATU RFP and branded campaign testing proposal represent the vendor selection decisions that will shape the next 2-3 years. Exelixis is building its zanzalintinib launch research infrastructure now. The vendors who win these bids get embedded in the launch; the vendors who lose must wait for re-entry opportunities that may not come until post-launch.

3. Integrated Insights Is the Competitive Moat

ZoomRx's strongest differentiation on this account is the ability to connect campaign impact data (active) with ATU data (proposed) and branded campaign testing data (proposed) into a single pre-launch intelligence platform. No vendor starting from zero can offer this. The "three-legged stool" pitch (market shaping effectiveness + market awareness + message optimization) is the narrative that wins.

Immediate Priorities

  1. Deliver exceptional quality on the Zanzalintinib Unbranded Campaign Impact study. This is the live proof point. Client satisfaction on this project is the most influential factor in the ATU and branded campaign testing decisions.

  2. Win the ATU RFP. This is the most strategically important open bid. It creates a multi-year tracking relationship and opens doors for PET, Launch KPI, and other services.

  3. Close the Branded Campaign Testing proposal. The iterative v0.1-to-v0.2 dialogue signals engagement. Understand what feedback drove the revision and whether a decision is imminent.

  4. Enrich the contact map. Pull names, titles, and emails from all project documents and RFP correspondence. Understanding the decision-making structure is essential for winning the current bids and expanding the relationship.

  5. Begin ATU-to-PET pipeline planning. If the ATU is won, the PET pitch should be ready to deploy within 60 days — "ATU tells you the market landscape; PET tells you whether your field force is winning within it."

  6. Monitor STELLAR-301 and STELLAR-302 clinical milestones. Trial readouts will accelerate (or delay) the zanzalintinib launch timeline and directly impact when launch-phase research (PET, Launch KPI, MSL) becomes relevant.

Project Timeline

# Project Date Service Brand Status
-- Zanzalintinib Branded Campaign Testing Apr 2026 Campaign/Message Testing Zanzalintinib Proposal (v0.2)
-- Zanzalintinib ATU Apr 2026 ATU Zanzalintinib RFP received
04 Zanzalintinib Unbranded Campaign Impact Feb 2026 Campaign Impact Zanzalintinib Active (W01 + W02)
03 Exelixis_Perxcept Jan 2024 Perxcept CABOMETYX Completed
02 Cabozantinib Integrated Tracking Oct 2023 Integrated Tracking CABOMETYX Completed
01 EXL_Cabo_PET_2019 2019 PET CABOMETYX Completed
00 AE Training 2023 Training Corporate Completed

Back to Accounts Hub | Home

Cross-Workstream Themes
1. The Cabo-to-Zanzalintinib Transition Is the Central Narrative
Every project on this account — past and future — maps to the cabozantinib-to-zanzalintinib franchise transition. The historical projects (PET, Integrated Tracking) served the mature franchise. The current and pipeline projects (campaign impact, ATU, branded testing) serve the pre-launch successor. Future opportunities (PET, Launch KPI, MSL) will serve the launch and post-launch phase. The account team should frame every proposal in the context of this transition.
2. The Current RFP Cycle Is a Defining Moment
The ATU RFP and branded campaign testing proposal represent the vendor selection decisions that will shape the next 2-3 years. Exelixis is building its zanzalintinib launch research infrastructure now. The vendors who win these bids get embedded in the launch; the vendors who lose must wait for re-entry opportunities that may not come until post-launch.
3. Integrated Insights Is the Competitive Moat
ZoomRx's strongest differentiation on this account is the ability to connect campaign impact data (active) with ATU data (proposed) and branded campaign testing data (proposed) into a single pre-launch intelligence platform. No vendor starting from zero can offer this. The "three-legged stool" pitch (market shaping effectiveness + market awareness + message optimization) is the narrative that wins.

Immediate Priorities


Deliver exceptional quality on the Zanzalintinib Unbranded Campaign Impact study. This is the live proof point. Client satisfaction on this project is the most influential factor in the ATU and branded campaign testing decisions.


Win the ATU RFP. This is the most strategically important open bid. It creates a multi-year tracking relationship and opens doors for PET, Launch KPI, and other services.


Close the Branded Campaign Testing proposal. The iterative v0.1-to-v0.2 dialogue signals engagement. Understand what feedback drove the revision and whether a decision is imminent.


Enrich the contact map. Pull names, titles, and emails from all project documents and RFP correspondence. Understanding the decision-making structure is essential for winning the current bids and expanding the relationship.


Begin ATU-to-PET pipeline planning. If the ATU is won, the PET pitch should be ready to deploy within 60 days — "ATU tells you the market landscape; PET tells you whether your field force is winning within it."


Monitor STELLAR-301 and STELLAR-302 clinical milestones. Trial readouts will accelerate (or delay) the zanzalintinib launch timeline and directly impact when launch-phase research (PET, Launch KPI, MSL) becomes relevant.
